title: Each document has a feed at ph:v2:doc:<id> pointing to a manifest that indexes operation batches on /bytes
content:
Each document on Swarm has a feed at topic `ph:v2:doc:<documentId>`. The feed points to a JSON manifest that indexes operation batches uploaded to `/bytes`:

```json
{
  "documentId": "abc-123",
  "documentType": "powerhouse/budget-statement",
  "latestRevision": { "global": 47 },
  "operationBatches": [
    { "reference": "a1b2c3d4...", "scope": "global", "branch": "main", "startIndex": 0,  "endIndex": 23, "timestamp": "..." },
    { "reference": "e5f6a7b8...", "scope": "global", "branch": "main", "startIndex": 24, "endIndex": 47, "timestamp": "..." }
  ],
  "encrypted": true,
  "updatedAt": "2026-04-07T10:05:00Z"
}
```

Each `reference` points to a `/bytes` upload containing the encrypted batch. `latestRevision` is keyed by scope, allowing multi-scope documents. `encrypted: true` signals the payload format; readers validate via the SWE prefix at `/bytes`.
description: Document manifest carries documentId, documentType, latestRevision per scope, and an operationBatches array with reference, scope, branch, startIndex, endIndex, and timestamp.
